Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a long neck. Because of this, many people began to believe in the monster, but even today we can't be certain. Looking at the options: A) found the monster itself - Hmm, the passage says they took pictures that seemed to show a monster, but it's not clear if they actually found the monster. The answer probably isn't A because it's not certain. B) found a huge cave under water - Wait, the huge cave was found earlier, in the part about using underwater television cameras. The 1975 group didn't find the cave, so B is not correct. C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all - The passage doesn't say that the scientists believed there wasn't a monster.
